meaning: adv.于是,因此 conj.在什么上面
examples: Mr. Jones refused to talk to them except in the company of his legal colleagues, whereupon the police officers departed.
Nibs exclaimed, aghast at such insubordination, whereupon Peter went sternly toward the young lady's chamber.
He told her she was a liar, whereupon she walked out.
Whereupon, the trial court held a bench trial wherein the Engineer was the sole witness.
You never really notice this until you leave the country, whereupon the white noise suddenly stops.
